name: Web Policy
description: Standard for declarative domain policy — the rules that govern whether an action is allowed, an entity qualifies, or an approval is required (“orders over $X need sign-off”, “a minor can't consent”). Distinct from Web Validation (input invalidity — a field is wrong): policy decides whether an action is permitted, a different concern with its own home. Adopts the OMG DMN vocabulary — decision tables + FEEL-style expressions — as the rule meta-schema (standardize the meta-schema, not the rule list), with rule versioning and per-context/tenant scoping. Composes the existing substrate — Web Expressions (conditional logic), Web Contexts (scoped policy flow), Web Validation, Web Traces (the proof point) — rather than absorbing into them. Rule authoring surfaces as a Technical Configurator domain (plateau-app). Foundation for the proof-of-compliance (tamper-evident decision log) and enforcement (PDP/PEP) slices.
tierEvidence: Frontier UI ships webpolicy/; plateau-app's conformance-engine consumes it (src/conformance-engine/embedSuites.ts) — shipped constellation use of the declarative-policy protocol.
